name: USS Kennebec (AO 36)
events: This convoy departed Port Said on 4 April 1944.

On departure from Port Said the convoy was made up of the merchant vessels; Anna Knudsen (Norwegian (tanker), 9057 GRT, built 1931), Burnside (British, 5659 GRT, built 1940), Caithness (British, 4970 GRT, built 1935), Dallington Court (British, 6889 GRT, built 1929), Egero (Norwegian (tanker), 7590 GRT, built 1929), Fort Bourbon (British, 7133 GRT, built 1942), Fort Dease Lake (British, 7126 GRT, built 1943), Fort Hudson's Hope (British, 7129 GRT, built 1942), Ima (Norwegian (tanker), 6842 GRT, built 1930), Moses Rogers (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Mount Revelstoke Park (Canadian, 7144 GRT, built 1943), Samfeugh (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samtay (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Shickshinny (American, 5103 GRT, built 1919) and William Harper (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943).
On departure from Port Said the convoy was escorted by the corvettes HMS Bergamot (A/Lt.Cdr. W. McInnes, RNR) and HMS Myosotis (T/Lt. R. Lugg, RNR).

On 5 April 1944, the following merchant vessels departed Alexandria to join the convoy; Anne Bradstreet (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Beaconsfield (British, 4635 GRT, built 1938), Chiswick (British, 6006 GRT, built 1943), Cid (British, 1847 GRT, built 1922), Corfell (British, 1802 GRT, built 1934), David Stone (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Edward Rowland Sill (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Empire Glory (British, 7290 GRT, built 1943), Empire Grange (British, 6981 GRT, built 1943), Eric V. Hauser (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Euryades (British, 5801 GRT, built 1913), Ferdinand Westdahl (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Fernplant (British, 5274 GRT, built 1939), Fort St. Paul (British, 7137 GRT, built 1943), Glenstrae (British, 9460 GRT, built 1922), Henry Dearborn (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), John L. Stoddard (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), John Page (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Joshua L. Chamberlain (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Riverton (British, 7307 GRT, built 1943), Russell A. Alger (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Tatra (Norwegian, 4766 GRT, built 1937) and Van der Capelle (Dutch, 7037 GRT, built 1942).
They were escorted by the frigate HMS Barle (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Davies, RNVR) (S.O. 48th Escort Group) and the corvette HMS La Malouine (Lt. W.A. Ives, RNR).

On 10 April 1944, the following merchant vessels were detached to Augusta; Beaconsfield, Burnside, Caithness, Chiswick, Corfell, Dallington Court, Empire Glory, Empire Grange, Euryades, Fort Bourbon, Fort Dease Lake, Fort Hudson's Hope, Fort St. Paul, Riverton and Van der Capelle.

The following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Augusta; Amberton (British, 5377 GRT, built 1928), Asa Gray (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Askeladden (Norwegian, 2496 GRT, built 1920), Belgian Airman (Belgian, 6960 GRT, built 1942), Benito Juarez (American, 7244 GRT, built 1943), Benjamin Goodhue (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), British Promise (British (tanker), 8443 GRT, built 1942), Comedian (British, 5122 GRT, built 1929), Corcrest (British, 2373 GRT, built 1918), Crosby S. Noyes (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Edward Richardson (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Empire Addison (British, 7010 GRT, built 1942), Empire Rosalind (British, 7290 GRT, built 1943), Eugene Hale (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Fort Brandon (British, 7131 GRT, built 1943), Fort Cataraqui (British, 7130 GRT, built 1942), Fort Michipicoten (British, 7152 GRT, 1943), Fort Remy (British, 7127 GRT, built 1943), Hardingham (British, 7269 GRT, built 1942), Helencrest (British, 5233 GRT, built 1941), John Clarke (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Joseph Hewes (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Joshua Seney (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Lyminge (British, 2499 GRT, built 1919), Maihar (British, 7563 GRT, built 1917), Mark Twain (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Mirabeau B. Lamar (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Nelson Dingley (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Neuralia (British, 9182 GRT, built 1912), Norman Monarch (British, 7005 GRT, built 1943), Ocean Trader (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942), Peter V. Daniel (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), R.C. Brennan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Roald Amundsen (Norwegian, 7191 GRT, built 1943), Robert Treat Paine (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Samythian (British, 7219 GRT, built 1944), Taria (Dutch (tanker), 10354 GRT, built 1939), Thomas B. Reed (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Thomas Lynch (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Thomas Sumter (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942) and Wiley Post (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943). The naval oil barge (self propelled) USS Pumper also joined the convoy.
On 10 April 1944, the merchant vessel Cid was detached to Malta.

The following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Malta; Balteako (British, 1328 GRT, built 1920), Belgian Veteran (British, 7174 GRT, built 1942), Graiglas (British, 4312 GRT, built 1940), Sameveron (British, 7219 GRT, built 1944) and Samkey (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943).

On 11 April 1944, the following merchant vessels were detached to Tunis / Bizerta; Empire Rosalind, Fort Brandon, Ima, Mark Twain, Ocean Trader, R.C. Brennan, Roald Amundsen and Thomas Lynch. USS Pumper was also detached to Bizerta.

The following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Tunis; Fylingdale (British, 3918 GRT, built 1924), George H. Thomas (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), John M. Harlan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Maya (Honduran, 5528 GRT, built 1929) and William Kent (American, 7187 GRT, built 1942).

The following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Bizerta; Cyrus W. Field (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Darien (Panamanian, 3683 GRT, built 1924), Hugh Williamson (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Pallade (Italian, 1152 GRT, built 1899) and Paul H. Harwood (American (tanker), 7192 GRT, built 1918).

At 1245 Z/11 the British escort was detached to Bizerta [apparently HMS Myosotis proceeded to Malta] and a new escort joined. This escort was made up of the destroyers USS Decatur (Lt. D.G. Wright, USNR, with COMTASKFOR 64, Capt. H.S. Berdine, USCG on board), USS Whipple (T/Lt.Cdr. S.E. Woodard, USN, with COMDESDIV 57, T/Cdr. E.W. Yancey, USN, on board), USS Alden (Lt. W. Herkness, 2nd, USN), USS John D. Edwards (Lt. R.A. Norelius, USNR) and the destroyer escorts USS Sellstrom (Cdr. W.L. Maloney, USCG, with COMCORTDIV 23, Cdr. F.P. Vetterick, USNR, on board), USS Ramsden (Lt.Cdr. S.T. Baketel, USCGR), USS Mills (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Muzzy, USCG), USS Rhodes (Cdr. E.A. Coffin, Jr., USCG), USS Richey (Cdr. P.DuP. Mills, USCG), USS Savage (Cdr. O.C. Rohnke, USCG), USS Tomich (Lt. C.B. Brown, USNR) and USS Sloat (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Cashman, USNR). As additional escort the AA cruiser HrMs Jacob van Heemskerck (Capt. W. Harmsen, RNethN) also joined the convoy.
At 0015 Z/12, USS Tomich fouled her rudder and screws with an obstruction and she was ordered to proceed to Oran to clear. She later rejoined at 1130 Z/14.
At 0500 Z/12, the following merchant vessels were detached to Bone; Belgian Airman, Belgian Veteran, Comedian, Empire Addison, Fort Michipicoten, Hardingham, Lyminge and Norman Monarch.

At the same time the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Bone; Empire Trent (British, 5006 GRT, built 1927), Everleigh (British, 5222 GRT, built 1930), Janine (French, 2345 GRT, built 1918) and Novasli (Norwegian, 3204 GRT, built 1920).
At 1030 Z/12, the merchant vessel Barrgrove (British, 5222 GRT, built 1918) joined the convoy coming from Philippeville.
At the same time, the merchant vessel Janine was detached to Bougie.

At 1200 Z/13, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Algiers; Atjutant (British, 1931 GRT, built 1922), Atlantic City (British, 5133 GRT, built 1941), Clan Cameron (British, 7243 GRT, built 1937), Clearpool (British, 5405 GRT, built 1935), Donacilla (British (tanker), 8113 GRT, built 1939), Edward N. Hurley (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943), Empire Shearwater (British, 4970 GRT, built 1920), Empire Symbol (British, 7027 GRT, built 1943), Fort Maurepas (British, 7133 GRT, built 1942), Joseph Alston (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Lesto (British, 1893 GRT, 1918), Morrison R. Waite (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Nyhorn (Norwegian, 4494 GRT, built 1929), Robert Jordan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Robert M.T. Hunter (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Samearn (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Taxiarchis (Greek, 4221 GRT, built 1913) and Walter E. Ranger (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943). Also joining from Algiers was the submarine HMS Uproar (Lt. L.E. Herrick, DSC, RN).
At the same time the following merchant vessels were detached to Algiers; Askeladden, Corcrest, Egero, Everleigh, Fort Remy, Glenstrae, Neuralia, Samythian and Thomas Sumter.

At 1130 Z/14, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Oran; Benjamin R. Milam (American, 7244 GRT, built 1943), Christopher Gadsen (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Dilworth (American (tanker), 7045 GRT, built 1920), Ezra Cornell (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Fanad Head (British, 5038 GRT, built 1941), Felipe de Neve (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Fort Frederick (British, 7135 GRT, built 1942), Fort Pine (British, 7133 GRT, built 1942), Francis L. Lee (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), George B. McClellan (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Henry Durant (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Isaac Sharpless (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), John B. Gordon (American, 7201 GRT, built 1943), M. M. Guhin (American, 7180 GRT, 1943), Mary Wilkins Freeman (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Niobe (Panamanian (tanker), 7299 GRT, built 1915), Samson (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943) and Tanafjord (Norwegian, 5922 GRT, built 1921). The naval stores ship USS Polaris (T/Cdr. C.R. Miller, USN) also joined the convoy. Also joining were the destroyer USS Lansdale (T/Cdr. D.M. Swift, USN) and the minesweeper USS Pilot (Lt.Cdr. S.B. Wetmore, USNR), which had escorted the joiners from Oran, joined the convoy. Also USS Tomich rejoined the convoy.
At the same time the following merchant vessels were detached to Oran; Adjutant, Crosby S. Noyes, Empire Trent, Lesto and Taxiarchis.
At 2000 Z/14, USS John D. Edwards left to convoy to proceed ahead to Gibraltar to fuel. She rejoined at 1108 Z/15.
At 0200 Z/15, USS Lansdale parted company with the convoy.
At 0240 Z/15, USS Whipple left to convoy to proceed ahead to Gibraltar to fuel. She rejoined at 1255 Z/15.
At 0920 Z/15, USS Alden left to convoy to proceed ahead to Gibraltar to fuel. She rejoined at 1800 Z/15.
At 1108 Z/15, USS John D. Edwards rejoined the convoy having fuelled at Gibraltar.
At 1255 Z/15, USS Whipple rejoined the convoy having fuelled at Gibraltar.
At 1315 Z/15, HrMs Jacob van Heemskerck left to convoy to proceed to Gibraltar.

At 1600 Z/15, the destroyer USS John D. Ford (T/Lt.Cdr. J.S. Slaughter, USN) joined the convoy coming from Gibraltar where she had been undergoing temporary repairs to repair damage sustained from a collision on 29 March.
At 1800 Z/15, USS Decatur left to convoy to proceed ahead to Gibraltar to fuel. She rejoined at 2045 Z/15. USS Alden had just rejoined the convoy having fuelled at Gibraltar.
At 1845 Z/15, HMS Uproar parted company to proceed to Gibraltar escorted by USS Mills.

At 1900 Z/15, the merchant vessel Joshua Henry (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943) joined the convoy coming from Gibraltar. She was escorted to the rendezvous by the patrol vessels USS PC-471 (Lt. G.B. Calkins, USNR) and USS PC-480 (Lt.(jg) J.K. Miller, USNR) which also joined the convoy.
At the same time the merchant vessels Amberton, Anna Knudsen, Balteako, Empire Shearwater, Fylingdale, Graiglas, Novasli and Pallade were detached to Gibraltar.
At 2045 Z/15, USS Decatur rejoined the convoy having fuelled at Gibraltar.
At 2200 Z/15, rejoined the convoy after having escorted HMS Uproar to Gibraltar.

At 1200 Z/16, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Casablanca; Frederick Banting (American, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Lorraine (French (tanker), 9512 GRT, built 1937), Newton D. Baker (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Pan-Maryland (American (tanker), 7701 GRT, built 1938), Sambay (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samchess (British, 7219 GRT, built 1944), Samota (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samovar (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943) and William J. Bryan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943). Also the naval tanker USS Kennebec (Cdr. M.D. Mullen, USNR) joined coming from Casablanca. They had been escorted to the rendezvous by the destroyers HMS Antelope (Cdr. J.G. Gould, RN), HMS Anthony (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Wallace, DSC, RN), HMS Brilliant (Lt.Cdr. J. Smallwood, RN), HMS Vanoc (Lt.Cdr. P.R. Ward, RN) and the patrol vessel USS PC-472 (Lt.Cdr. P.A. Dallis, Jr., USNR). These escort vessels temporary joined the convoy.

At 1800 Z/16, the following merchant vessels parted company with the convoy to proceed to Casablanca; Barrgrove, Clearpool, Empire Symbol, Fort Cataraqui, Fort Frederick, Nyhorn, Robert M.T. Hunter, Samearn and Sameveron. USS PC-471, USS PC-472 and USS PC-480 went with them as escorts. The four British destroyers which had joined at 1200 Z/16 also parted company in accordance with their orders.

At 0700 Z/17, the merchant vessel Niobe straggled from the convoy. She was ordered to continue on the the USA via the stragglers route but later information was received that she was ordered to proceed to the Azores.
On 19 April 1944, USS Decatur and USS Whipple fuelled from USS Kennebec.
On 20 April 1944, USS John D. Ford, USS John D. Edwards, USS Alden and USS Pilot fuelled from USS Kennebec.
At 1045 Z/20, the merchant vessels Fort Anne (British, 7134 GRT, built 1943) and Hoke Smith (American, 7199 GRT, built 1943) joined the convoy coming from the Azores.
On 23 April 1944, USS Decatur fuelled from USS Kennebec.
On 27 April 1944, USS Decatur, USS Whipple, USS Alden, USS John D. Edwards and USS John D. Ford fuelled from USS Kennebec.

At 1300 Z/29, USS Kennebec parted company with the convoy to proceed to Bermuda. The destroyer escorts USS Edmonds (T/Cdr. C.S. Barker, Jr., USN) and USS Shelton (Lt.Cdr. L.G. Salomon, USNR) had just made rendezvous with the convoy to escort the tanker to her destination.

At 0001 Z/1, the Hapton Roads section of the convoy, made up of 38 merchant vessels parted company. USS Whipple, USS Alden, USS John D. Edwards, USS John D. Ford and USS Pilot went with them as escorts.
At 1100 Z/1, the Delaware Capes section of 5 merchant vessels was detached. USS Ramsden and USS Savage went with them as escorts.
At 2100 Z/1, two merchant vessels were detached to St. John, New Brunswick.
At 0250 Z/2, one merchant vessel was detached to Philadelphia. The remainder of the convoy entered New York harbour later that morning. (2)
This convoy departed Hampton Roads on 23 May 1944.

It was made up of the following transports / tankers; Arthur P. Davis (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Asa Gray (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Atlanta City (American, 5686 GRT, built 1921), Belgian Airman (Belgian, 6960 GRT, built 1942), Benjamin Goodhue (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Charles Carroll (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Christopher Gale (American, 7177 GRT, built 1943), Cistula (Dutch (tanker), 8097 GRT, built 1939), City of Florence (British, 6862 GRT, built 1918), Clausina (British (tanker), 8083 GRT, built 1938), Edward N. Hurley (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943), Edward Richardson (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Empire Garrick (British (tanker), 8128 GRT, built 1942), Ezra Cornell (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), F.A.C. Muhlenberg (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Felipe de Neve (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Ferdinand Westdahl (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Fernplant (British, 5274 GRT, built 1939), Fort George (British, 7129 GRT, built 1942), Francis L. Lee (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943), Frederick Banting (American, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Garonne (Norwegian (tanker), 7113 GRT, built 1931), George B. McClellan (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Gutzon Borglum (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Haym Salomon (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Heimvard (Norwegian, 4851 GRT, built 1930), Henry Gilbert Costin (American, 7200 GRT, built 1943), Hiram S. Maxim (American, 7194 GRT, built 1943), Isaac Sharpless (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), J.L. Luckenbach (American, 6369 GRT, built 1919), James Gunn (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943), John Barton Payne (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), John Clarke (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), John H. Eaton (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), John M. Harlan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Joseph Alston (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Joseph Hewes (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Joshua Seney (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Knute Nelson (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Lorraine (French (tanker), 9512 GRT, built 1937), M.M. Guhin (American, 7180 GRT, 1943), Mary Wilkins Freeman (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Mirabeau B. Lamar (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Moses Rogers (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Nelson Dingley (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Nelson W. Aldrich (American, 7176 GRT, built 1944), Newton D. Baker (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Ninella (British (tanker), 8134 GRT, built 1943), Norrisia (British (tanker), 8246 GRT, built 1944), Northia (British (tanker), 8211 GRT, built 1944), Pan-Maryland (American (tanker), 7701 GRT, built 1938), Pierre Laclede (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Regent Lion (British (tanker), 9551 GRT, built 1937), Renald Fernald (American, 7176 GRT, built 1944), Robert Treat Paine (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Samfeugh (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samglory (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944), Samleyte (British, 7255 GRT, built 1944), Samothrace (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samovar (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samtay (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samtrust (British, 7209 GRT, built 1943), Samvigna (British, 7255 GRT, built 1944), Shickshinny (American, 5103 GRT, built 1919), Steel Inventor (American, 5686 GRT, built 1920), Sul Ross (American, 7247 GRT, built 1944), Sun (American (tanker), 9002 GRT, built 1928), Sverre Helmersen (Norwegian, 7209 GRT, built 1944), Tatra (Norwegian, 4766 GRT, built 1937), Thomas Pinckney (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Trondheim (Norwegian (tanker), 8258 GRT, 1939), Victor Herbert (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Walter E. Ranger (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Wiley Post (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), William Bradford (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), William H. Wilmer (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943), William Harper (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943) and William J. Bryan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943).

The naval tanker USS Kennebec (Cdr. M.D. Mullen, USNR) was also part of the convoy as were the following LCI(L)'s; USS LCI(L)-590 (Lt.(jg) T.E. Boland, USNR), USS LCI(L)-591 (Lt.(jg) W.A. Putnam, Jr., USNR), USS LCI(L)-592 (Lt.(jg) A.H. Swanke), USS LCI(L)-593 (Lt.(jg) B.V. Wilson, USNR), USS LCI(L)-594 (Lt.(jg) W. Campbell, USNR), USS LCI(L)-595 (Lt.(jg) C. Ringle, Jr., USNR), USS LCI(L)-596 (Lt.(jg) W.H. Crawford, USNR), USS LCI(L)-674 (Lt.(jg) W.P. Eckel, USNR), USS LCI(L)-675 (Lt.(jg) J.F. Tobin, USNR), USS LCI(L)-951 (Lt. N.A. Thompson, USNR), USS LCI(L)-952 (?), USS LCI(L)-953 (Lt.(jg) D.J. Stadfeld, USNR) and USS LCI(L)-954 (Lt.(jg) J.K. Ullrich, USNR).

On departure from Hampton Roads the convoy was escorted by Task Force 64 which was made up of the destroyer USS McCormick (T/Cdr. F.A. Brock, USN, with COMTASKFOR 64, Capt. H.S. Berdine, USCG, on board), USS Sellstrom (Cdr. W.L. Maloney, USCG, with COMCORTDIV 23, Cdr. F.P. Vetterick, USNR, on board), USS Ramsden (Lt.Cdr. S.T. Baketel, USCGR), USS Mills (Lt.Cdr. V. Pfeiffer, USCG), USS Rhodes (Cdr. E.A. Coffin, Jr., USCG), USS Richey (Cdr. P.DuP. Mills, USCG), USS Savage (Lt.Cdr. R. Ridgely, 3rd, USCG), USS Fowler (Lt.Cdr. G.S.J. Forde, USNR), USS Durik (T/Lt.Cdr. K.B. Smith, USN), USS Tomich (Lt. C.B. Brown, USNR), USS Earl V. Johnson (Lt.Cdr. J.J. Jordy, USNR).
Around 2200 Z/25, the destroyer escort USS Wiseman (Lt.Cdr. W.B. McClaran, Jr., USNR) joined the convoy escort followed an hour later by her sister ship USS Solar (Lt.Cdr. H.A. Hull, USNR).
During 27 May 1944, USS Kennebec fuelled USS McCormick, USS Solar, USS Wiseman, USS Fowler, USS Durik and USS Earl V. Johnson.
During 31 May 1944, USS Kennebec fuelled USS McCormick, USS Solar, USS Wiseman, USS Earl V. Johnson, USS Fowler and USS Durik.

Around 0410 Z/3, the auxiliary minelayer USS Planter (Lt.Cdr. T.T. Scudder, Jr., USNR) joined the convoy coming from the Azores. She had been escorted to the rendezvous by the auxilary A/S trawler HMS Kingston Amber (T/Lt. R. Adams, RNR) which did not join the convoy but set course to return to the Azores taking the transport Joseph Hewes with her.

Around 1335 Z/5, USS Kennebec was detached from the convoy to proceed to position 25°00'N, 20°00'W to fuel other USN ships. She was escorted by USS Durik. Before the tanker parted company she fuelled USS McCormick, USS Solar, USS Wiseman, USS Fowler, USS Durik, USS Earl V. Johnson and USS Planter.
Around 2000 Z/5, USS LCI(L)-952 staggled from thee convoy due to engine trouble. She rejoined the convoy around 1630 Z/6.

Around 1800 Z/7, the following ships were detached to Casablanca; Charles Carroll, John Barton Payne, Joseph Alston, Lorraine and Samfeugh. To escort them there the patrol vessels 472, 473 and 482 had come out from Casablanca and had joined the convoy around 1630 Z/7. The Casablanca section arrived at its destination the following day.
Around 1400 Z/8, USS McCormick parted company with the convoy to pick up liason officers at Gibraltar. She rejoined around 1547 Z/8.
Around 1830 Z/8, the AA cruiser HMS Colombo (Capt. C.T. Jellicoe, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) joined the convoy.
Around 0805 Z/9, the minesweeper USS Seer (Lt.Cdr. H.S. Walker, Jr. USNR) joined the convoy for jamming duties (against German radio guided bombs).

Around 1830 Z/9, the Oran section of the convoy parted company. This was mad up of the following ships; Asa Gray, Belgian Airman, Ezra Cornell, Garonne, John Clarke, Newton D. Baker and Pan-Maryland. At the same time two ship joined the convoy coming from Oran, these were the following; Chloris (British, 1171 GRT, built 1910) and George Bancroft (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942).

Around 1930 Z/9, the destroyer USS Madison (T/Cdr. D.A. Stuart, USN) and the minesweeper USS Sustain (Lt. J.E. Lindeman, Jr., USNR) joined the convoy for jammer duty (against German radio controlled bombs).

Around 1530 Z/10, the Algiers section of the convoy parted company. This was mad up of the following ships; Christopher Gale and Clausina. At the same time two ship joined the convoy coming from Algiers, these were the following; Portsea (British, 1583 GRT, 1938) and Zaanstroom (Dutch, 1646 GRT, built 1920).
At 0318 Z/11, USS Tomich was detached to proceed back towards Algiers to land an urgent hospital case there. She rejoined the convoy around 2350 Z/11.
Around 2030 Z/11, USS Madison and USS Sustain parted company with the convoy.
At 0353 Z/12, USS Ramsden parted company with the convoy to proceed ahead to Bizerta to land the remains of a deceased crew member.
Around 0500 Z/12, HMS Colombo parted company to proceed to Bizerta.

Between 0715 Z/12 and 0800 Z/12, the following ships parted company with the convoy to proceed to Bizerta; Hiram S. Maxim, J.L. Luckenbach, Norrisia and Portsea. Also USS Planter and all 13 LCI(L)'s entered Bizerta as did the following escort vessels; USS McCormick, USS Sellstrom, USS Mills, USS Rhodes, USS Richey, USS Savage, USS Fowler, USS Tomich and USS Earl V. Johnson.
The following ships joined the convoy off Bizerta; Auk (British, 1338 GRT, built 1921), Black Hawk (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943) and John C. Breckinridge (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943).

Also a new escort joined off Bizerta, this was made up of the frigate HMS Barle (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Davies, RNVR), HMS Bryony (T/Lt. T. Hand, RNR), HMS Jonquil (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.W. Tretheway, RNR) and HMS Myosotis (T/Lt. R. Lugg, RNR).
On 13 June 1944, the Chloris arrived at Malta after having been detached from the convoy while the transport Palermo (British, 2797 GRT, built 1938) joined the convoy coming from Malta.

On 13 June 1944, the following ships departed Augusta to join the convoy; British Confidence (British (tanker), 8494 GRT, built 1936), British Tradition (British (tanker), 8443 GRT, built 1942), Califonia (American (tanker), 10398 GRT, built 1921), Dilworth (American (tanker), 7045 GRT, built 1920), Empire Lionel (British, 7030 GRT, built 1942), Empire Peak (British, 7045 GRT, built 1943), Fort Aklavik (British, 7132 GRT, built 1943), Fort Frontenac (British, 7148 GRT, built 1943), Malvina (Dutch (tanker), 8249 GRT, built 1932), Norelg (Norwegian, 6103 GRT, built 1920), Nuculana (British (tanker), 8179 GRT, built 1942), Ocean Messenger (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942), Ocean Traveller (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942), Ocean Vesper (British, 7174 GRT, built 1942), Samoa (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Sampan (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samphire (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samwash (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Stancleeve (British, 5970 GRT, built 1942) and Tide Water Associated (American (tanker), 8906 GRT, built 1930).

On 14 June 1944 the following ships arrived at Augusta after having parted company with the convoy; Auk, Benjamin Goodhue, Black Hawk, Cistula, Edward N. Hurley, Edward Richardson, Empire Garrick, F.A.C. Muhlenberg, Felipe de Neve, Fort George, Francis L. Lee, George B. McClenllan, George Bancroft, Haym Salomon, Isaac Sharpless, John C. Breckinridge, John B. Eaton, Joshua Seney, M.M. Guhin, Mirabeau B. Lamar, Moses Rogers, Ninella, Northia, Regent Lion, Robert Treat Paine, Steel Inventor, Sun, Thomas Pinckney, Thomas Sumter, Trondheim, Walter E. Ranger, William H. Wilmer and Zaanstroom.

On 17 June 1944, the following ships arrived at Alexandria; Empire Lionel, Fernplant, Fort Aklavik, Fort Frontenac, Ocean Messenger, Ocean Vesper, Palermo, Samglory, Samleyte, Samphire and William Harper. Also the following escort ships arrived at Alexandria; HMS Barle and HMS Bryony.
The remainder of the convoy arrived at Port Said on 19 June 1944.
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
